constraints_inherited_from_nano_vm_ecosy: NO eval()/exec() anywhere — ASTEngine only (applies to M3+ Program conditions)
ruff line-length=100, CI check only, no --fix (recursive trigger)
ASTEngine: string literals NOT supported as RHS in CONDITION — use numeric sentinel (0/1)
LiteLLMAdapter: direct import from nano_vm.adapters.litellm_adapter — not via __init__
OPENAI_API_KEY + OPENAI_API_BASE set BEFORE litellm import
Terminal tool = single PG transaction, external calls (HTTP/MQ) FORBIDDEN inside tx block
GovernanceEnvelope FORBIDDEN to stub in M1/M2 — cargo cult, implement only at M3
fsm_instances table FORBIDDEN — current_state lives in entity primary table
Direct state mutation (order.status = X) FORBIDDEN outside terminal tools
transition() signature: event= parameter only, never new_state=
Transactional patching for dev-agent: stage_patch → validate_staged_mypy(tmpdir) → commit_patches → run_pytest → git_checkout_files on failure
S&R |matches|=1 invariant — read current file before patching

sieshka_specific_decisions.point: LightweightTrace uses same trace_id field name as future nano-vm Trace — chosen deliberately for M3 migration ease, not a coincidence
OrderFSM constructed with injected state_reader/state_writer callbacks rather than direct DB import — keeps FSM unit-testable without database, repository wraps callbacks in sprint_m1_postgres_wiring
order_programs.py and order_tools.py drafted ahead of M3 (in M1 scaffold) as design reference only — NOT wired, NOT executed. Prevents architecture drift between M1 custom FSM and M3 target shape.

ADR-005 (provider fallback via FSM, not gateway) and ADR-006 (YandexGPT primary/GigaChat fallback) added to ARCHITECTURE.md v1.3 based on validated provider-fallback-demo pattern from nano-vm ecosystem DECISIONS.md 2026-06-17 entry
